text: The surgeon moves the spleen from lateral to medial and identifies the tail of the pancreas, mobilizing it to access the adrenal gland. Fat tissue is separated from the right crus, and the adrenal gland is dissected and mobilized. A lateral to medial resection of the adrenal gland is performed.
The surgeon ligates all pedicles during the dissection, identifies and clips the left adrenal vein, and places the adrenal gland specimen in an endopouch for retrieval.
